depends_on: MARKET_RESOLVED_DB_FIELDS_001 must be APPROVED (both touch bot_core.py)
purpose: Add hard per-session and daily max-loss kill switches. If realized losses since session start exceed SESSION_MAX_LOSS_USD, or realized losses today exceed DAILY_MAX_LOSS_USD, block all new entries until the operator restarts or the next calendar day. These caps prevent uncontrolled drawdown when the model is wrong repeatedly. Audit found no such caps exist anywhere in the codebase.

context.finding: RISK_PIPELINE_AUDIT_REPORT_001.md — gaps table, severity HIGH: 'No per-session max loss cap, no daily max loss cap found in risk/loop'
context.existing_mechanism: SL cluster detection (core/risk.py:55-63) triggers a loop-level cooldown after N stop-losses in 5 minutes — this is a recovery pause, not a hard cap. It does not prevent re-entry after the cooldown expires regardless of total session loss.
required_behavior: If sum(pnl_usd for closed trades since session_start) <= -SESSION_MAX_LOSS_USD: block all new entries, log ERROR, do not exit open positions
If sum(pnl_usd for closed trades today UTC) <= -DAILY_MAX_LOSS_USD: same block
Caps are configurable via .env — SESSION_MAX_LOSS_USD and DAILY_MAX_LOSS_USD
If env vars are absent or 0, caps are disabled (safe default for backward compatibility)

step1_env_vars.where: bot_core.py — near the existing env var reads at the top of the file

step2_session_start_tracking.where: bot_core.py — record session start timestamp at process startup, before the main loop
step2_session_start_tracking.note: This is a module-level global set once at startup. Not a DB write.

step3_loss_cap_check_function.where: bot_core.py — add a helper function near the other helpers
add: session_pnl = sum(t.pnl_usd for t in closed if t.pnl_usd and t.ts_close and int(t.ts_close) >= _session_start_ts)
logger.error('SESSION LOSS CAP HIT: session_pnl=%.2f limit=%.2f — blocking all new entries', session_pnl, SESSION_MAX_LOSS_USD)
daily_pnl = sum(t.pnl_usd for t in closed if t.pnl_usd and t.ts_close and int(t.ts_close) >= today_start)
logger.error('DAILY LOSS CAP HIT: daily_pnl=%.2f limit=%.2f — blocking all new entries', daily_pnl, DAILY_MAX_LOSS_USD)
step3_loss_cap_check_function.note: datetime and timezone are already imported in bot_core.py. fetch_recent_closed is already imported. Confirm these before writing.

step4_gate_in_entry_loop.where: bot_core.py — entry loop, before the model bridge/signal evaluation block. Specifically before the section that calls check_entry_gates(). The check should short-circuit the entire entry evaluation for the loop iteration.
add: pass  # cap logged inside helper, skip entry evaluation this loop
step4_gate_in_entry_loop.note: Use 'pass' or 'continue' on the outer loop depending on structure — do not exit open-position monitoring. Loss caps block entries only, not exit checks.

alternative_approach.description: If the above helper is too complex to wire cleanly, an acceptable alternative is to add a check inside check_entry_gates() in core/risk.py as an additional gate. Pass session_pnl and daily_pnl as arguments, check against caps there. Either approach is acceptable — the worker should choose whichever is cleaner given the actual code structure seen in the file.
alternative_approach.note: If implementing in core/risk.py, the cap values must be read from env vars inside risk.py and the function signature must be extended. Do not pass raw DB queries into risk.py — compute the pnl sums in bot_core.py and pass the float.

steps: git diff --name-only  # must show only allowed files
grep -n '_session_loss_exceeded\|SESSION LOSS CAP\|DAILY LOSS CAP' bot_core.py  # confirm helper and log lines
Logic trace: with SESSION_MAX_LOSS_USD=0 confirm helper returns False immediately (caps disabled)

do_not: Close open positions when caps are hit — caps block entries only
Reset or modify the DB when caps are hit
Hard-code cap values — they must come from SESSION_MAX_LOSS_USD and DAILY_MAX_LOSS_USD env vars
Touch dashboard_server.py, core/paper_exec.py, or any file not in allowed_files
Change TP/SL thresholds or any existing gate logic
Start this task before MARKET_RESOLVED_DB_FIELDS_001 is APPROVED
